title: cotula sessilis
intro: Cotula sessilis, also known as the bog button or marsh button, is a small, annual herb native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cotula sessilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and soothe digestive issues. Cotula sessilis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile ingredient in traditional herbal remedies.
